# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an Istio AuthorizationPolicy that controls access to services. Create rules to allow traffic from specific service accounts. Configure source and operation matching for HTTP methods and paths. This is for our microservices access control.
# Category: istio_authz_bypass

apiVersion: "security.istio.io/v1beta1"
kind: "AuthorizationPolicy"
metadata: 
 name: 'allow-all'   # Unique policy id, can be any string or number to identify the rule in Istio traffic management system (e.g., Kiali)
 namespace: <namespace>    # Replace with your actual Namespace if it is different from default one i.e "default" 
spec:  
 targetSpans: - '*'     # All spans match this rule, in other words all services are matched as targets of the policy by Istio traffic management system (like Kiali)   
 selector: {}          # Selector for selecting which service or set of microservices to apply rules. If left empty it will select everything 
 action: ALLOW     # Allowed/Deny access type, can be either "ALLOW" OR DENY". The policy is allowed by default if not specified here  
 rule {    # Define the match and actions in case of allow or deny traffic from specific service accounts. It's optional to define rules for HTTP methods & paths as well  (e.g., httpMethod: GET, pathRegex : /service/*)    
 - name: '**'        # Select all destinations/services which can be matched by this rule  
    match: {          // Define the criteria of destination service to allow or deny traffic from specific accounts and methods  (e.g., sourceAccounts, httpHeaders{headers})     
       sourceAccounts: - '*'     # Allows access for any account/service in which case it means all services are allowed by default  
        mTLS: true    // Apply this rule only to TLS traffic  (e.g., secure service communication)        
    }
